hard.next_step: Brief the Head of Procurement and agree a way forward; offer a HOP-level summary/pitch/demo. Ball in buyer's court since 5 May 2026 demo.
hard.ais_why: Buyer's project owner (controllership/procurement excellence team) is pushing broader automation and agentic AI across Oracle processes; product scope includes ANA (Autonomous Negotiation). AI mandate is buyer-originated but posture is pre-commitment (BD unengaged, demo high-level, ball in buyer court as of 5 May).

north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery (28 Apr) and a high-level demo (5 May) both landed well with Zermeena Khan and her procurement excellence team, but the buyer has not returned the promised Head-of-Procurement session dates and has gone quiet for 62 days; no budget exists, Finance has never engaged, and Careem is weighing whether to just fix Oracle internally.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Apr 2026, Qualified same day (67 days in stage as of 6 Jul). Discovery 28 Apr, high-level demo 5 May. Last Salesforce activity 25 May (rep Eid email, 42 days ago); last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 5 May demo (62 days ago). Close date 31 Dec 2026 is 178 days out and aligns with the buyer's stated end-2026 digitization deadline, so the date is not the problem. The problem is momentum: the agreed next step (brief HOP, return demo dates) has not moved in 62 days and the buyer's own June go-live target has already lapsed. Owner reassigned Dan Quinn -> Mohamad Alhakim (RVP) on 29 Jun, a reset signal. Without buyer re-engagement inside 14 days and a HOP session inside 30 days, this drifts toward Off Track.
evidence: Genuine, buyer-stated pain on discovery 28 Apr 2026: newly rolled-out Oracle eSourcing has low adoption because the UI is too complex for non-tech sourcing managers and, above all, for suppliers who must be trained onto it. Zermeena Khan: 'a large part of the sourcing tool is where the suppliers need to be trained... that's where I think the greatest challenge is.'

Zermeena Khan called the fit strong on discovery 28 Apr 2026: 'This was very useful. In fact, very, very close to our notice of veranda... one part is source to contract and the other is purely operational.'

Buyer pulled the demo themselves on 28 Apr ('we can have the demo in the next day or two... if you have one next week') and Zycus delivered a high-level demo on 5 May, received well (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026).
Only stated feature gap post-demo: Careem wants to advertise opportunities to the market as Jaggaer does with public-sector clients (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026).

Agreed next step (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026): brief the Head of Procurement and agree a way forward; Zycus offered a HOP-level pitch/demo. Ball left in the buyer's court, chase if nothing heard in a week or two.

62 days of buyer silence since the 5 May demo. Only rep-side outreach followed (Agentic AI Summit invites to Kashan Ahmed and Zermeena Khan on 8 May; Eid email 25 May); no buyer reply, no HOP dates, no internal approval signal.

No budget exists. Zermeena Khan on 28 Apr: 'it really depends on the budget'; any budget would be 'part of the process' built after evaluating the solution. Decision is in-house to procurement, with Finance/budget-owner approval secondary.
Economic buyer not engaged: Ashish Jain (Senior Vice President, Finance), the SFDC Executive Sponsor and likely budget owner, has never appeared on a call or activity.

Primary threat is the status quo / internal build: Zermeena floated fixing it internally ('we could rather do that through internal capability by creating a board that can maneuver the Oracle ERP... or explore a whole different tool altogether', 28 Apr). Oracle is both the incumbent being displaced and the fallback.

Buyer's own June go-live target for a usable, digitized sourcing tool has already passed with no digitization, and they stated a hard deadline not to exit 2026 without it (Next_Step__c IMPACT, 5 May 2026).

metrics.narrative: The business problem is sharp but unquantified. On discovery 28 Apr 2026 Zermeena Khan described 'double pressure' to digitize historically manual, email-based quote collection AND make the newly rolled-out Oracle eSourcing tool actually usable, or adoption and digitization targets fail. She named supplier onboarding/training on a complex tool as 'the greatest challenge'. Careem put no numbers on it: no cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings figure was offered. Zycus cited only generic benchmarks (Dan Quinn quoted 3-10%, ~6% savings on addressable spend, 28 Apr), which Careem never validated. Closing this means co-building the light business case Dan offered so the adoption and savings impact is quantified for Finance.

economic_buyer.narrative: The buying authority is clear in shape but the person is not engaged. Zermeena Khan (Senior Procurement Manager) said on 28 Apr 2026 that this is 'a completely in-house decision, completely around control [procurement]... We firstly have to be convinced that this is the way to go, and then getting the approvals through the authorities and the relevant budget owners would be secondary.' So procurement drives the choice and Finance holds the budget gate. SFDC names Ashish Jain (Senior Vice President, Finance) as the Executive Sponsor, making him the most likely economic buyer / budget owner, but he has never joined a call or activity and there is no established Finance thread. The move is to have Zermeena introduce Ashish Jain and validate the budget path before any commercial step.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ashish Jain, SVP Finance, Executive Sponsor

decision_criteria.narrative: Careem stated its criteria clearly on 28 Apr 2026. The dominant criterion is usability for BOTH internal sourcing managers and suppliers: Zermeena Khan, 'the interface has to be user friendly, easy to maneuver, and Oracle is clearly not all check marks on that department.' Second is cost/efficiency: they will pick 'the most economical option with the best efficiency' and are explicitly running a can-we-vs-should-we tradeoff against building internally. Third is Oracle fit: they asked for a demo relevant to what they already run in Oracle and for integration complexity and timeline. A specific feature criterion also surfaced post-demo: the ability to advertise opportunities to the market as Jaggaer does for public-sector clients (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026). Formal weighted criteria are not yet documented; these are the buyer-stated ones.

decision_process.narrative: The process is readable from the call itself. The procurement/controllership excellence team owns and drives it: Zermeena Khan convened her whole team for discovery on 28 Apr (Kashan Ahmed on automation/AI, a data analyst, and later Abida Hani on contracts and vendor onboarding). The stated sequence is procurement must first be convinced, then internal approvals go 'through the authorities and the relevant budget owners' (Finance). The agreed next gate post-demo (Next_Step__c 5 May 2026) is to brief the Head of Procurement and agree a way forward, with Zycus offering a HOP-level pitch/demo. So the path is working-team validation -> Head of Procurement buy-in -> Finance/budget approval. The Head of Procurement above Zermeena is not yet named, and that HOP session is the stuck gate.

paper_process.narrative: Nothing on contracting mechanics has surfaced, which is normal for a Qualified deal with no commercials yet. There is no discussion of MSA/SOW, procurement channel, legal redlines, or InfoSec timing. Careem does have the functions that will drive later paper: Abida Hani (Contracts Management) and Srijith Nair (Chief Information Security Officer) both sit in the account and the CISO will own any security review. There is no contract-expiration forcing function (Oracle eSourcing was only just rolled out). Visibility here is created by reaching commercials and asking Zermeena Khan to map Careem's internal procurement, legal, and InfoSec approval chain.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Abida Hani (Contracts Management), Srijith Nair (CISO)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and buyer-owned. On 28 Apr 2026 Zermeena Khan explained Careem has just launched Oracle eSourcing but adoption is low because the interface is too complex for non-tech sourcing managers and, critically, for suppliers who must be onboarded and trained onto the tool, which she called 'the greatest challenge'. In parallel they are still digitizing historically manual, email-based quote collection. Kashan Ahmed added the team is already building agentic-AI workflows so 'anything that has anything to do with Oracle can be done by AI', underscoring an automation gap. The pain owner is Zermeena Khan and the procurement excellence team. Inferred but not buyer-confirmed pain (tail-spend leakage, multi-jurisdiction contract complexity) came from Dan Quinn's hypothesis slide, which Careem called 'pretty much accurate' but did not elaborate.
sources: Avoma discovery call 28 Apr 2026 (Zermeena Khan, Kashan Ahmed)

champion.narrative: Zermeena Khan (Senior Procurement Manager) is the lead engaged contact and de-facto coach: she initiated contact at the Dubai conference, convened her full team for discovery on 28 Apr, drove the agenda, praised the fit ('very, very close to our need'), and pulled the demo herself. Kashan Ahmed (procurement, automation/AI initiatives) is the AI advocate who validated Careem's agentic-AI direction. What has developed is genuine working-level engagement and a stated solution fit. What is still missing is any evidence either is selling Zycus upward: the agreed HOP briefing has not happened in 62 days, neither has returned demo dates, and there is no proof of access to or advocacy with Finance. The champion is therefore developing and at risk, not yet a true internal seller.
sources: Avoma discovery call 28 Apr 2026 (Zermeena Khan, Kashan Ahmed)

competition.narrative: There is no active rival S2P vendor in a bake-off; the real contest is the status quo and internal build. Careem just deployed Oracle eSourcing and is openly weighing whether to persevere with Oracle (train and extend it) or build a simpler interface in-house: Zermeena Khan, 28 Apr 2026, 'we could rather do that through internal capability by... creating a board that can maneuver the Oracle ERP system on the backend, or explore a whole different tool altogether'. Oracle is thus both the incumbent being displaced for its poor UX and the low-effort fallback. Jaggaer surfaced only as a feature benchmark (public-sector market advertising), not an active evaluation. Zycus wins by positioning as an Oracle-complementary UX and AI layer (Oracle stays the ERP system of record), leading with Autonomous Negotiation (ANA) and guided Intake, and quantifying time-to-value versus a slow internal build against Careem's end-2026 deadline.

deal_movement.summary: Two solid intro touches (discovery 28 Apr, high-level demo 5 May) then 62 days of silence. Only rep-side outreach followed (Agentic AI Summit invites 8 May, Eid email 25 May). Owner reassigned Dan Quinn -> Mohamad Alhakim (RVP) on 29 Jun, a reset signal, not buyer engagement.

items.change: Discovery call held; Careem surfaced Oracle eSourcing UI/adoption pain and a live agentic-AI mandate
Stage moved Initial Interest -> Qualified; Amount set to 150000
High-level demo delivered on priority areas (iSupplier/iSource, touched Intake and ANA), received well
Rep-side re-engagement attempts (Agentic AI Summit invites to Kashan and Zermeena); no buyer reply
Last Salesforce activity: rep Eid Mubarak email; no buyer response
Owner reassigned Dan Quinn -> Mohamad Alhakim (Regional Vice President) by Carl Kimball

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is do-nothing / internal build, not a rival vendor. Careem just rolled out Oracle eSourcing and on 28 Apr 2026 Zermeena Khan floated fixing or building on top of Oracle in-house rather than buying, and has since gone quiet for 62 days, so status-quo may be winning by default. Oracle eSourcing is the incumbent being displaced for poor UX yet is also the low-effort fallback. Jaggaer appears only as a feature benchmark (market advertising, 5 May 2026), not an active evaluation. No competing S2P suite (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP) has been named by the buyer.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / internal build on Oracle
competitors.quote: We could rather do that through internal capability by, you know, creating a board that can maneuver the Oracle ERP system on the backend or explore a whole different tool altogether... obviously, being the procurement team, we have to think of the most economical option with the best efficiency.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify Zycus time-to-value, lower risk, and supplier-portal usability versus a slow in-house build against Careem's end-2026 deadline; position Zycus as build-partner (iSaaS Oracle integration), not build-vs-buy.

competitors.name: Oracle eSourcing (incumbent)
competitors.quote: The Oracle interface is too complex for non-tech sourcing managers and for suppliers... usability and adoption of the live Oracle tool are already challenged.
competitors.source: Next_Step__c SITUATION/IMPACT 5 May 2026; discovery call 28 Apr 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as an Oracle-complementary UX/AI layer (Oracle stays ERP system of record) with far easier eSourcing, supplier onboarding, and guided Intake; lead ANA and Intake as the step-change; cite Bolt (UAE ride-hailing) as a regional Oracle-integrated reference.

competitors.name: Jaggaer
competitors.quote: Only gap I can see at this point is that they want to advertise their opportunities to the market, as Jaggaer does with public sector clients.
competitors.how_we_win: Confirm Zycus can support or roadmap public-facing opportunity advertising so it does not become an objection; treat Jaggaer as a feature benchmark, not an active competitor.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Careem's stated expectations centre on usability for suppliers and buyers, cost-efficiency versus building internally, and Oracle fit. Zycus aligns strongly on usability and AI, is exposed on cost/budget (none secured) and on one feature (market advertising).

items.criterion: UI easy for sourcing managers AND suppliers to use/onboard
items.quote: The interface has to be user friendly, easy to maneuver, and Oracle is clearly not all check marks on that department.
We are working on agentic AI workflows where AI is actually reading documents for us, doing actions for us in the Oracle system... anything that has anything to do with Oracle can be done by AI.

items.criterion: Most economical option vs building internally
items.quote: It really depends on the budget... we have to think of the most economical option with the best efficiency, and what's the easier, most beneficial interface.

items.criterion: Advertise sourcing opportunities to the market (as Jaggaer does)
items.quote: Only gap... they want to advertise their opportunities to the market, as Jaggaer does with public sector clients.

items.requirement: Share Oracle and Slack integration complexity, effort, and typical timeline
items.quote: It would really help us if you can also share... the time it takes to integrate and the complexity that you faced, especially around Oracle and Slack.

items.requirement: Confirm ability to advertise sourcing opportunities to the market like Jaggaer
items.quote: Only gap I can see at this point is that they want to advertise their opportunities to the market, as Jaggaer does with public sector clients.

items.deliverable: Provide business-case / ROI support (light spreadsheet model or detailed analyst-built case) to help justify budget
items.grounding_quote: As far as the business case goes, we can do two things. One is a very light... spreadsheet... The more granular... a series of analysts... So we can do one or the other.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 28 Apr 2026 (Dan Quinn); Next_Step__c 5 May 2026 'Option to support the business case/ROI if required'

items.deliverable: Return date options and brief the Head of Procurement, then agree a way forward
items.grounding_quote: Next step is to brief HOP and agree a way forward, have offered to provide a summary/pitch/demo to HOP - ball is in their court.

items.quote: Careem articulated adoption and usability pain but gave no cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings figures; only generic Zycus benchmarks were mentioned.
Ashish Jain (SVP Finance), the likely budget owner, has never appeared on a call or activity; budget path unproven.

items.area: Head of Procurement identity and buy-in
items.quote: The agreed HOP briefing has not happened; the Head of Procurement above Zermeena is unnamed and unengaged.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal with real fit but stalled momentum; the win levers are re-igniting the buyer against their own deadline, multi-threading to power, and beating the do-nothing/internal-build option.
flags: Re-engage Zermeena Khan against Careem's own end-2026 digitization deadline to unstick the Head-of-Procurement session, the one gate blocking progress.
Multi-thread beyond the procurement excellence team: build a Finance thread to Ashish Jain (SVP Finance) and identify the Head of Procurement, since sign-off runs through both and neither is engaged.
Neutralize the do-nothing / internal-build threat by quantifying Zycus time-to-value and supplier-onboarding ease versus fixing Oracle in-house, the tradeoff Zermeena explicitly named.

champion_strength.summary: Zermeena Khan (Senior Procurement Manager) is the primary engaged coach: she initiated contact at the Dubai conference, convened her full excellence team for discovery on 28 Apr 2026, drove the agenda, praised the fit ('very, very close to our need'), and pulled the demo. Kashan Ahmed supports as the automation/AI advocate. What has developed is strong working-level engagement and a stated solution fit; what is missing is any internal selling upward, evidence of access to Finance, and momentum, since the agreed Head-of-Procurement briefing has not happened in 62 days. Developing and at risk.
champion_strength.champion: Zermeena Khan (Senior Procurement Manager); Kashan Ahmed (automation/AI advocate)
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 28 Apr 2026; Next_Step__c 5 May 2026; SF last activity 25 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus positioned the AI story well: Dan Quinn led with the Merlin agentic stack, the Autonomous Negotiation agent (ANA), and guided Intake, which mapped directly onto Careem's own agentic-AI mandate (Kashan Ahmed described building agentic workflows on Oracle). ANA is in the product scope. The narrative landed; it now needs to be carried in front of the Head of Procurement and Finance to convert.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 7 / Moderate, driven by a buyer-originated AI mandate. Calls show the appetite is if anything stronger than 'Moderate': Kashan Ahmed said the team is already building agentic-AI workflows so 'anything that has anything to do with Oracle can be done by AI', and the project owner is pushing broader automation across controllership. The AI signal is real and slightly under-rated by the field.

items.detail: No budget secured; spend is contingent on building a business case after evaluating the solution.
Economic buyer Ashish Jain (SVP Finance) not engaged and the Head of Procurement not briefed; sign-off chain unmapped.
Do-nothing / internal-build risk: Careem may persevere with Oracle or build a simpler interface in-house rather than onboard a second vendor.
Buyer's own June go-live target for a usable, digitized sourcing tool has lapsed with no digitization and no re-engagement.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling. Strong two intro touches then 62 days of buyer silence, no HOP dates, no budget, no Finance thread; only rep-side outreach and an owner reassignment on the Zycus side.
items: 62 days silent since the 5 May demo; agreed HOP briefing not executed.
Only rep-side outreach since (invites 8 May, Eid email 25 May), no buyer reply.
Owner reassigned to RVP Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun, a reset rather than buyer-driven momentum.

items.trigger: 62 days silent since the 5 May demo; agreed HOP briefing not executed
items.expected_effect: Restarts momentum and reopens the one gate (HOP buy-in) blocking progress toward the close date.

items.expected_effect: Clears two open buyer asks so they are not objections, and gives Zermeena material to brief the HOP.

items.expected_effect: Converts the high-level fit into a validated use-case demo and advances toward ShoeFit/BRD and commercials.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay suite explored on discovery (spend analysis through sourcing, CLM, supplier risk/performance, eProcurement, eInvoicing)
scope_change.to: Source-to-Contract phase 1: eSourcing plus supplier management and guided Intake/ANA (iSource, iSupplier, iSaaS, ANA), with CLM and supplier risk/performance deferred to later phases
scope_change.detail: Budget-driven, defensive phasing: Zermeena Khan said 'it really depends on the budget' and wants the most economical option, starting with eSourcing and building from there ('take this step by step'); Zycus offered to phase high-priority modules first, so scope narrowed from the full S2P vision to an S2C phase-one footprint.

customer_preference.evidence: Zermeena Khan praised the fit on 28 Apr ('very, very close to our need') and Careem pulled the demo, but no Zycus-specific commercial next step was driven and the buyer went silent for 62 days; positive but not decisive.

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (Oracle UI too complex, low adoption, supplier onboarding the biggest challenge, hard end-2026 deadline) but no customer-quantified cost, savings, or ROI; only generic Zycus benchmarks mentioned.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Careem has real, buyer-stated pain and a live agentic-AI mandate that fit Zycus well, but 62 days after the 5 May demo the deal is silent with no budget, no engaged economic buyer, and the buyer weighing whether to just fix Oracle internally.

win_position.text: Zycus fits Careem's stated need: Zermeena Khan called the demo 'very, very useful, very close to our need' on 28 Apr and named Oracle eSourcing's UI complexity and supplier onboarding as the core pain, with an end-2026 digitization deadline as the forcing event.
There is no funded buyer: Zermeena said on 28 Apr the decision 'really depends on the budget' and any budget must be built after evaluating the solution, so nothing is committed.
The economic buyer Ashish Jain (SVP Finance) has never joined a call and the Head of Procurement has not been briefed 62 days after the demo, so there is no path to sign-off.

deal_momentum.text: The deal has been silent for 62 days since the 5 May demo; the agreed step to brief the Head of Procurement and return demo dates has not happened and only rep-side emails have gone out.
Careem showed real intent when engaged: Zermeena pulled the demo herself on 28 Apr ('we can have the demo in the next day or two') and named a firm end-2026 deadline.
Ownership was reset from Dan Quinn to RVP Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun, a sign the deal needed intervention rather than organic progress.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment stops at two intro sessions: no budget, no procurement approval, no signed next step, and the ball was left in the buyer's court and dropped.
Zermeena Khan brought her full excellence team (Kashan Ahmed, contracts, a data analyst, later Abida Hani) to discovery on 28 Apr, signalling internal seriousness at the working level.

deal_risk.text: The primary threat is do-nothing / internal build: Zermeena floated fixing or building a simpler interface on Oracle in-house rather than buying, and 62 days of silence suggests status-quo may be winning by default.
Careem's own June go-live target has already lapsed with no digitization, so the buyer's stated timeline is slipping.

deal_momentum.evidence: 62 days of buyer silence post-demo; agreed HOP briefing not executed; only rep-side outreach; owner reset 29 Jun.

customer_commitment.evidence: No budget, no approval, no signed next step; ball left in buyer's court and dropped.

deal_risk.evidence: Active do-nothing / internal-build option plus a lapsed self-imposed June deadline.

critical_signals.text: The real contest is do-nothing: Careem is weighing whether to just fix or build on top of the Oracle tool it already rolled out rather than buy, and has gone quiet on the decision since 28 Apr.
The 5 May demo landed well and Zermeena Khan called the fit very close to their need, but she never returned the promised Head-of-Procurement session dates in the 62 days since.
No budget exists yet: Zermeena said any spend depends on building a business case after evaluating the solution, and Finance has not engaged.
Economic buyer Ashish Jain (SVP Finance) is named in Salesforce but has never joined a call, so the approval path is unmapped and unengaged.

Economic buyer (Ashish Jain, SVP Finance) and the Head of Procurement have never engaged; budget authority unproven.
Contracting/paper process not yet visible (deal is early, Qualified).
